Japanese DeBERTa V2 base model pretrained on 171 GB of Japanese Wikipedia, CC‑100 and OSCAR data using Juman++ segmentation and SentencePiece tokenization, trained for three weeks on eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs and ready for fine‑tuning.
Fine‑tuned Japanese Hubert‑Base for CTC phoneme recognition (v4) with updated sentence‑filtering rules, pronunciation adjustments, and a GPU switch to A6000, stopped training at 110k steps.
s: Speech Recognition, Speech & Audio in NLP

d: The repository delivers new Japanese text‑embedding v3 models (30M‑310M params, 8 k‑token max, JMTEB 74‑77) that can be loaded with sentence_transformers (using query/passage prefixes) and includes benchmark comparisons with supervised/unsupervised SimCSE, LaBSE, E5, and other models.

LLM‑jp‑4‑VL 9B beta is a 9‑billion‑parameter Japanese vision‑language model built on llm‑jp‑4‑8b‑instruct and SigLIP‑2, employing InternVL3.0‑style dynamic tiling and a lightweight MLP projector, and achieves competitive performance on Japanese benchmarks after training on FineVision and Jagle.

Japanese Sentence‑LUKE model trained on the same dataset as Sentence‑BERT, outperforming or matching it, built on studio‑ousia/luke‑japanese‑base‑lite and used via Hugging Face Transformers’ MLukeTokenizer and LukeModel.

PLaMo Translation Model is a large‑scale language model created by Preferred Networks for translation tasks, available in base, post‑trained, and evaluation variants, released under the PLaMo community license and not instruction‑tuned for chat or other downstream uses.
FuguMT is a Japanese‑to‑English Marian‑NMT translation model built with transformers and SentencePiece, scoring 39.1 BLEU on Tatoeba.
s: Multilinguality, Text Generation, Machine Translation, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: Anime‑XCodec2‑44.1kHz‑v2 upsamples 16 kHz Japanese speech to 44.1 kHz high‑fidelity audio with a decoder‑only RMS‑loss fine‑tune, keeping the encoder/codebook frozen and preserving identical speech tokens.

Fine‑tuned from PaddleOCR‑VL, PaddleOCR‑VL‑For‑Manga achieves 70 % full‑sentence accuracy on Manga109‑s speech‑bubble crops—over triple the 27 % baseline—using a multi‑language dataset and includes training code and a developer guide.
High‑performance Japanese SPLADE v2 enables sparse‑vector conversion and inference through a WebUI demo, trains with YAST, offers YASEM embedding, and reports JMTEB benchmark results.

A Qwen 3.5‑9B GGUF model fine‑tuned for Japanese, employing Unsloth Dynamic Quantization 2.0, extensive bug fixes, large‑scale Japanese calibration, and runnable on CPU with 16 GB RAM and 6 GB disk via llama.cpp.
A gguf‑formatted version of cyberagent’s Llama‑3.1‑70B‑Japanese‑Instruct‑2407, built using TFMC/imatrix‑dataset-for‑japanese‑llm data and run with llama.cpp’s CLI.

A T5 v1.1 Japanese encoder‑decoder model, trained on Japanese Wikipedia and mC4/ja with GEGLU activation, dropout off, larger d_model but fewer heads, 512‑token inputs, 114‑token outputs, 256‑batch fp32, CC‑BY‑SA 4.0 licensed for commercial use upon contact.
A collection of weighted‑/imatrix and static GGUF quantizations for the Aurora‑SCE‑12B‑v2 model, including download links, size/quality rankings, and usage guidance.
Container for a gguf‑format conversion of llm‑jp‑4‑8b‑instruct, built from imatrix data, with LMStudio settings and example llama.cpp CUDA commands.

Japanese DeBERTa V3 base pre‑trained on 540 B tokens from LLM‑jp v1.0, trained with a modified DeBERTa V3 setup, uses a unigram byte‑fallback tokenizer (no morphological analyzer), and is fine‑tuned for JGLUE NLU tasks.
GGUF‑format conversion of llm‑jp‑4‑32b‑a3b‑thinking, built with TFMC’s imatrix dataset, for CUDA‑enabled inference via llama.cpp.

JMTEB is a Japanese text‑embedding benchmark featuring 5 tasks (clustering, classification, STS, retrieval, reranking) and 28 datasets, offering a one‑line evaluation script and inviting community contributions.
s: Information Extraction & Text Mining, Responsible & Trustworthy NLP, Representation Learning, Text Classification, Semantic Text Processing

d: JMedBench is a Japanese biomedical LLM benchmark comprising 20 datasets across five tasks (MCQA, NER, STS, etc.) sourced from MedMCQA, PubMedQA, MMLU, and others, each with its own license, and includes a note that translations may contain biases requiring human review.

Nemotron‑Personas‑Japan is an open‑source, CC BY 4.0 dataset of high‑quality, synthetically generated Japanese personas—incorporating name, gender, age, background, marital status, education, occupation and location—grounded in real‑world demographic, geographic and personality distributions, engine

A mirror of the Reazon Speech v2 Japanese dataset hosted on Hugging Face, distributed under CDLA‑Sharing‑1.0 with use restricted to Japanese Copyright Act Article 30‑4, containing 4,096 16 kHz FLAC audio files and matching transcriptions in TSV/CSV format.
Japanese‑Eroge‑Voice‑V2 offers 2,657 hours of anonymized 1,033,142 eroge audio–transcription pairs (mostly female, NSFW), MIT‑licensed for academic research.

WAON is a large, high‑quality Japanese image‑text pair dataset built with size, SigLIP‑score filtering and deduplication (by URLs, captions and pHash), and released on HuggingFace under Apache 2.0 for informational analysis.
Dataset card for the Japanese MC4 dataset (mc4-ja).

A collection of Japanese large‑language models (1.8 b to 172 b beta1, with instruct variants) from NII’s R&D Center, packaged in Hugging Face Transformers format and pretrained on a mix of Japanese, English, and Web corpora totalling >1 trillion tokens, requiring torch ≥ 2.3, transformers ≥ 4.40, ac

Llama 3.1 Swallow is a set of 8‑B and 70‑B models that continue pre‑training Meta’s Llama 3.1 to boost Japanese language performance, then instruction‑fine‑tune on synthetic Japanese data—providing multiple released variants with improved conversational behavior comparable to gemma‑3‑27b‑it.

Qwen3‑Swallow v0.2 delivers Japanese‑English LLMs (30B‑A3B and 32B) trained via CPT, SFT and RLVR, preserving strong math, coding, and reasoning abilities, with nine released models and AWQ‑quantized variants.

A 6‑layer, 512‑hidden transformer called Japanese GPT‑2 xSmall, trained on Japanese CC‑100 and Wikipedia with SentencePiece tokenization, released Aug 25 2021 under MIT and hosted on Hugging Face (rinna/japanese‑gpt2‑xsmall) and cited in arXiv 2404.01657.
s: Syntactic Text Processing, Text Segmentation, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: A 3.6‑B‑parameter Japanese GPT‑NeoX model—36‑layer, 2816‑dim hidden transformer—trained by PPO‑based RLHF on translated Anthropic data, follows instruction‑style conversation, shows ~30 % human‑and 34 % ChatGPT‑based PPO win over SFT, but tends to generate repeated text.
s: Dialogue Systems & Conversational Agents, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: A large Japanese GPT‑2 model trained by ABEJA, Inc. on Japanese CC‑100, Wikipedia, and OSCAR, tokenized with sentencepiece, usable via Hugging Face transformers in PyTorch or TensorFlow under the MIT license.
s: Text Generation, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: SB Intuitions’ Sarashina2.2‑0.5B instruct v0.1 is a 0.5‑billion‑parameter Japanese autoregressive model that performs well on Japanese and English MT benchmarks and is ready to load via torch‑transformers.

ELYZA‑japanese‑Llama‑2‑7b is a 6.27‑B‑parameter Japanese extension of Meta’s Llama‑2‑7B, further pre‑trained for Japanese language tasks and offered in base, instruct, fast, and fast‑instruct variants, maintained by the ELYZA team under the Llama 2 Community License.
TinySwallow‑1.5B‑Instruct is a 1.5 B Japanese instruction‑tuned autoregressive language model distilled with TAID from Qwen2.5‑32B‑Instruct, intended for research use only.
ELYZA‑japanese‑Llama‑2‑7b is a 6.27‑B‑parameter extension of Meta’s Llama‑2 model, pre‑trained on Japanese data with instruct and fast variants, and usable through Hugging Face Transformers.
Japanese 3.6 B‑parameter GPT‑NeoX model fine‑tuned for instruction‑following (SFT‑v2), evaluated against the prior SFT with ChatGPT on 100 prompts, released March 31 2023.
s: Dialogue Systems & Conversational Agents, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: OpenCALM is a family of Japanese decoder‑only Transformer language models (160 M–6.8 B parameters) from CyberAgent, trained on Japanese Wikipedia and Common Crawl and released under CC BY‑SA 4.0.
s: Syntactic Text Processing, Text Segmentation, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: Japanese RoBERTa‑base pretrained on Japanese Wikipedia and Japanese CC‑100, using Juman++ word segmentation and SentencePiece tokenization, trained in a week on 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs with Adam (lr = 1e‑4, native AMP), and fine‑tunable with results reported on JGLUE.
Japanese DeBERTa‑V2 large model pre‑trained on 171 GB of Japanese text (Wikipedia, CC‑100, OSCAR) with Juman++ segmentation, trained 36 days on 8 A100 GPUs, and fine‑tunable for downstream tasks.

reazonspeech-nemo-v2 is a 619‑M‑parameter Japanese long‑form ASR model built on an improved Fast‑Conformer with Linearly Scalable Attention, trained on the ReazonSpeech v2.0 corpus, offering multi‑hour inference via a subword RNN‑T decoder (3000‑token SentencePiece) and distributed under Apache 2.0.
s: Speech Recognition, Text Generation, Speech & Audio in NLP, Multimodality

d: Fine‑tuned Wav2Vec2‑Large‑XLSR‑53 for Japanese, trained on Common Voice and JSUT corpora with 16 kHz audio, ready for 16 kHz Canton usage and evaluation.
s: Speech Recognition, Speech & Audio in NLP

d: Ruri releases Japanese general text‑embedding models v3 (30 M–310 M params, 8192‑token max length) with top JMTEB scores, provides straightforward sentence_transformers usage (prefixed with 「クエリ: 」or 「文章: 」) and benchmark results for retrieval, STS, classification, reranking, clustering and pair‑cla
s: Representation Learning, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: Sarashina‑Embedding‑v1‑1B is a 1.2 B‑parameter Japanese text‑embedding model built on Sarashina2.1‑1B, trained with multi‑stage contrastive learning to reach state‑of‑the‑art scores on JMTEB while producing 1,792‑dimensional dense vectors for semantic similarity, search, and classification under a n

fio‑base‑japanese‑v0.1 is a proof‑of‑concept Japanese embedding model fine‑tuned from cl‑tohoku/bert‑base‑japanese‑v3 on limited data with a single GPU, released for textual entailment and retrieval tasks.

sbert-jsnli‑luke‑japanese‑base‑lite is a 768‑dimensional sentence‑transformer built on studio‑ousia/luke‑japanese‑base‑lite, trained one epoch on shunk031/jsnli, and includes examples for clustering, semantic search, and both Sentence‑Transformers and HuggingFace usage.
s: Representation Learning, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: fastText is a lightweight, open‑source library that rapidly learns word and sentence embeddings and classifiers on a standard CPU, trains on billions of words in minutes, can be compressed for mobile use, and provides pre‑trained vectors for classification and language identification.

A Japanese CrossEncoder reranker suite—including xsmall, small, base, large, and japanese‑bge‑reranker‑v2‑m3‑v1—paired with example usage, evaluation metrics on several benchmarks, and supporting documentation.
Japanese-language CrossEncoder reranker models—from xsmall to large—trained on Japanese text, exposed through sentence_transformers, with evaluation on JQaRA, JaCWIR, MIRACL, and JSQuAD.
A Japanese LUKE model fine‑tuned on the WRIME dataset that classifies which of eight emotions—joy, sadness, anticipation, surprise, anger, fear, disgust, trust—is expressed in a sentence.
s: Language Models, Emotion Analysis, Sentiment Analysis

d: Fine‑tuned Japanese BERT (cl‑tohoku/bert‑base‑japanese‑v2) on Amazon product reviews for sentiment classification, achieving ~81 % accuracy and 0.73 F1 after 6 epochs with a 2 × 10⁻⁵ learning rate.
s: Representation Learning, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing, Sentiment Analysis

d: Manga OCR is a Vision Encoder‑Decoder system that delivers high‑quality OCR of Japanese manga—including vertical and horizontal text with furigana overlays—across diverse fonts and low‑quality images, and can also be used for general printed Japanese OCR.

Fine‑tuned XLM‑RoBERTa‑base on a Japanese NER corpus (tags PER, ORG, LOC, INS, PRD, EVT) using 5‑epoch Adam (lr 5e‑5, batch 12) to reach a 0.0173 validation loss, released on Transformers 4.23.1 and PyTorch 1.12.1.
s: Multilinguality, Information Extraction & Text Mining, Cross-Lingual Transfer, Named Entity Recognition, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: A Japanese‑focused DeBERTa V3 model with xsmall, base and large variants that uses no morphological analyzer at inference, respects word boundaries, employs a reduced vocabulary (BPE only in the large model), and is Hugging Face‑compatible.

A Japanese TTS model named **tsukuyomi‑wavlm**—fine‑tuned for 300 epochs on 100 utterances from the tsukuyomi corpus, using the WavLM discriminator and A1/A2/A3 prosody features on a VITS architecture, exported as a 61 MB ONNX file that generates 22.05 kHz syntheses.
Offering a range of GGUF quantizations for the NeonMaid‑12B‑v2 model, it supplies download links, size/quality tables, usage guidance, and a FAQ for requests.
Llama‑3‑ELYZA‑JP‑8B is a Japanese‑enhanced 8‑B Llama 3 model with GGUF (Q4_K_M) and AWQ quantization, ready to run via llama.cpp, LM Studio, or an OpenAI‑compatible API.

A gguf‑converted Llama‑3.1‑Nemotron‑Nano‑8B‑v1 model built from the imatrix dataset, accompanied by instructions for compiling llama.cpp with CUDA and running the model via llama‑cli with a sample cooking prompt.

Provides weighted/iMatrix GGUF quantizations for IBM’s Granite Guardian 4.0‑3B Japanese toxicity model—from imatrix 0.1 up to i1‑Q6_K—with size/quality notes and download links hosted on mradermacher’s Hugging Face page.
Provides weighted/imatrix GGUF quantizations of the Anime‑Speech‑Japanese‑Refiner model, listing quant types and sizes, a comparison graph, and usage guidance for the vision‑model files.
GGUF‑format conversion of rinna’s qwen2.5‑bakeneko‑32b‑instruct‑v2 model built with TFMC/imatrix‑dataset‑for‑japanese‑LLM, with usage instructions using llama.cpp and optional CUDA support.

Provides a gguf‑converted version of cyberagent’s Mistral‑Nemo‑Japanese‑Instruct‑2408 model (built with imatrix data) and includes llama.cpp CUDA‑enabled build and usage instructions with a sample prompt.

A GGUF‑quantized shisa‑v2.1‑qwen3‑8b model built with Unsloth Dynamic 2.0, community‑patched Qwen3 settings to reduce malfunctions, a larger imatrix for stronger Japanese performance, and a 40K maximum context length.

AXCXEPT’s gguf‑formatted phi‑4‑open‑R1‑Distill‑EZOv1 model, built from the TFMC/imatrix‑dataset for Japanese LLM, can be run with CUDA‑enabled llama.cpp as shown in the provided build and launch commands.

Gemma‑2‑Llama‑Swallow‑9b‑it‑v0.1 in GGUF format, released by tokyotech‑llm and built from TFMC/imatrix‑dataset‑for‑japanese‑llm, is run with llama.cpp (CUDA‑enabled) using a prompt such as “You are a professional chef, teach me a recipe.”

A 31‑Billion‑parameter Japanese flagship model built on Gemma‑4‑31B‑it, released as the fused “akira‑papa‑1.0‑31b‑jp” with GGUF Q8_0 and Q4_K_M variants, optimized for long‑form blog/note writing, tech explanations, and light coding support while preserving natural Japanese tone.

A 100‑sample Japanese instruction‑tuning evaluation dataset of annotated tasks—ranging from summarization correction and math reasoning to translation, creative generation, and user‑intent understanding—designed for manual or automatic 5‑point rating of fine‑tuned models.

Japanese Anime Speech Dataset V2 delivers 292,637 cleaned audio‑text pairs—about 397.5 h of SFW and 52.4 h of NSFW content—in 128‑kbps MP3 files split by safety, designed specifically for training automatic speech‑recognition models.
s: Speech Recognition, Text Generation, Speech & Audio in NLP, Multimodality, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: Japanese JaQuAD, a subset of QG‑Bench, provides sentence‑ and paragraph‑level data with highlighted answer tokens for training Japanese question‑generation models, evaluated by BLEU4, METEOR, ROUGE‑L, BERTScore, and MoverScore.

FineWeb2 Edu Japanese delivers ~120 million high‑quality educational Japanese texts (≈89.3 billion tokens) from FineWeb2, filtered by a DeepSeek‑API classifier (score ≥ 2.5), tokenized via ModernBERT‑Ja‑130M, and includes a small‑token subset (≤512 tokens).
s: Information Extraction & Text Mining, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: A Japanese‑to‑English parallel corpus translating the kaken subset of llm‑jp‑corpus‑v3 with Qwen/Qwen2.5‑32B‑Instruct, featuring custom translation columns and licensed under CC‑BY‑4.0.

Pre‑training corpora for scaling data‑constrained Japanese LLMs, comprising organic Japanese/English web datasets (9B‑token JA‑WEB‑9B, 63B‑token EN‑WEB‑63B/JA‑WEB‑63B) and synthetic sets (Qwen3‑14B, JA‑PARAPHRASE‑63B, JA‑INSTRUCT‑63B, JA‑TRANSLATE‑63B) used in the EACL 2026 study.
Japanese GPT‑2 lyric generation model trained on 143,587 uta‑net songs, offered via a web demo and Torch/Transformers usage example.
s: Responsible & Trustworthy NLP, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing, Low-Resource NLP

d: GGUF‑formatted NVIDIA‑Nemotron‑Nano‑9B‑v2‑Japanese, rebuilt from the imatrix dataset, and ready to run with llama.cpp on CUDA.

ELYZA‑japanese‑Llama‑2‑7b is a Japanese‑optimized extension of Meta’s Llama‑2 7 B, offering instruct and fast variants with 6.27–6.37 B parameters that can be accessed via the Hugging‑Face Transformers library.

LLM‑jp‑3.1‑13b‑instruct4 is a 13‑B, instruction‑pre‑trained Japanese language model developed by NII’s R&D Center, released as a Hugging‑Face Transformers checkpoint with a UNIGRAM‑byte‑fallback tokenizer.
Japanese 150‑M‑parameter instructional LLM from Japan’s National Institute of Informatics, available as a Hugging Face‑compatible Transformer model requiring torch ≥ 2.3.0 and transformers ≥ 4.40.1.
RakutenAI‑7B‑instruct, built on Mistral‑7B‑v0.1, is a Japanese‑focused LLM that tops Japanese benchmark scores while matching English performance, and a chat‑tuned version is also released.
s: Syntactic Text Processing, Language Models, Text Normalization

d: A T5‑base summarization model fine‑tuned on the Japanese Livedoor news corpus, showcased in Chapter 7 of the Large‑Scale Language Model Introduction guide.

RakutenAI‑7B‑chat is a Japanese LLM built on the Mistral‑7B‑v0.1 architecture, delivering top benchmark results for Japanese language understanding and competitive performance on English, with both a foundation model (RakutenAI‑7B) and an instruction‑tuned version (RakutenAI‑7B‑instruct).

Sarashina2‑7B is a 7 B‑parameter Llama‑2 model trained on ~1 T Japanese Common Crawl tokens and SlimPajama English data with RoPE‑based architecture, yet it remains instruction‑unfined and may generate biased or inaccurate outputs.

jinen‑v1‑small is a 110 M‑parameter GPT‑2 language model fine‑tuned with BPE tokenization for high‑performance Japanese kana‑to‑kanji conversion, trained on preprocessed Miwa‑Keita zenz‑v2.5 data, and released in PyTorch and GGUF formats with context‑aware Unicode token markers.
Provides GGUF‑formatted, hardware‑quantised model files for Stability AI’s 70‑billion‑parameter Japanese StableLM Instruct Beta, ready for use with LLaMA‑cpp‑based tools.
DiaFill is a fine‑tuned Japanese two‑speaker dialogue script generator that produces natural, filler‑rich spoken‑style conversations from seed prompts, trained on proprietary GENIAC data.
OpenCALM is a suite of Japanese decoder‑only language models from CyberAgent, spanning 160 M to 6.8 B parameters built on GPT‑NeoX and released under CC BY‑SA 4.0.

A 12‑layer, 768‑dimensional BERT‑Base Japanese model trained on 2.6 GB of Wikipedia (≈17 M sentences), using IPA‑dictionary character tokenisation with whole‑word masking, and released under CC‑BY‑SA 3.0.

A Japanese BigBird base model pretrained on Japanese Wikipedia, CC‑100, and OSCAR—requiring Juman++ word segmentation and trained with Transformers/DeepSpeed on 16 A100 GPUs for 600 k steps—can be fine‑tuned on downstream tasks like JGLUE.
A ModernBERT‑base model trained on the 3.4 TB Japanese llm‑jp‑corpus v4, fine‑tuned in two stages (max_seq_len 1024 → 8192), achieves 0.92 JSTS, 0.91 JNLI, and 0.88 JCoLA.
Kotoba Whisper v2.0 is converted to CTranslate2 format for use with CTranslate2 and faster‑whisper, providing installation, inference examples, Apple M2 benchmarking, and conversion instructions.
s: Speech Recognition, Speech & Audio in NLP

d: Kotoba‑Whisper v1.1 is a Japanese ASR model that extends kotoba‑whisper‑v1.0 with a seamless punctuation‑adding post‑processing pipeline, improving transcription accuracy and reducing latency compared to multiple Whisper baselines.
s: Speech Recognition, Speech & Audio in NLP

d: Includes Ruri v3 Japanese text embeddings (30 M–310 M parameters, 8192‑token limit, JMTEB 74.5–77.2), instructions for Sentence Transformers using “クエリ:” or “文章:” prefixes, and benchmark results for several Japanese models such as Sup/Unsup SimCSE, GLuCoSE, and LaBSE.
s: Representation Learning, Language Models, Semantic Text Processing

d: A Japanese BERT‑base model fine‑tuned with supervised SimCSE on JSNLI, exposed via Sentence‑Transformers or HuggingFace with CLS pooling, trained on 1 M examples at 512‑batch size, 5 × 10⁻⁵ learning rate, 5 × 10⁻⁵ temperature, 64‑token limit, and BFloat16 precision.

Sarashina‑Embedding‑v2‑1B is a 1,792‑dimensional Japanese sentence transformer trained with multi‑stage contrastive learning that achieves state‑of‑the‑art JMTEB scores, and can be used for semantic similarity, search, paraphrase mining, classification, and clustering via Sentence‑Transformers with

Japanese CrossEncoder Reranker models (xsmall, small, base, large, BGE‑v2 m3) with hidden sizes 384–1024, example inference via sentence_transformers and Hugging Face, scoring 0.71–0.97+ on JQaRA, JaCWIR, MIRACL, and JSQuAD.
A Japanese cross‑encoder reranker built with Sentence Transformers that demonstrates inference usage and benchmark results for various sized Ruri‑Reranker models.

Japanese‑reranker‑small‑v2 is a lightweight, fast Japanese reranker model series (v2) offering tiny to base variants that reach up to 0.89 average score with 2–15 s GPU inference, plus cross‑encoder options, and requires Hugging Face Transformers v4.48+ with optional Flash Attention 2 for accelerati

LFM2‑350M‑ENJP‑MT is a fine‑tuned LFM2‑350M checkpoint that delivers near real‑time, bidirectional Japanese/English translation for short‑to‑medium inputs with quality comparable to models over ten times larger, as illustrated across everyday, technical, business, and news domains, and emphasizes co
FuguMT is a Marian‑NMT based English‑to‑Japanese translation model built with Hugging Face Transformers and SentencePiece, achieving a BLEU score of 32.7 on Tatoeba.

Sarashina2.2‑OCR is a 3‑B parameter, end‑to‑end OCR model that, after human‑preference optimization, parses Japanese and English documents into Markdown while converting tables to HTML, math to LaTeX, and graphics into bounding‑box annotations, and achieves this by integrating a SigLIP2‑based vision

A GGUF‑format release of pfnet’s plamo‑2‑translate built from imatrix data based on the TFMC/imatrix‑dataset‑for‑japanese‑LLM, with instructions to compile and run it via llama.cpp on CUDA‑enabled hardware.

Anime‑Speech‑Japanese‑Refiner is a Qwen3‑Omni 30B fine‑tuned model that processes Japanese anime/game speech audio to produce detailed descriptions (emotion, profile, etc.) and refined transcriptions with non‑speech events, trained on the Galgame Gemini Captions dataset, and runs best with vLLM.
GGUF‑converted Llama‑3‑ELYZA‑JP‑8B by elyza, built with the TFMC/imatrix‑dataset‑for‑japanese‑LLM, ready for use with llama.cpp.
A GGUF‑quantized version of the llm‑jp‑4‑8b‑thinking model that maintains a minimal chat template, usable via llama‑cpp’s llama‑completion or LM Studio but not with llama‑cli/llama‑server.
GGUF‑formatted version of HODACHI’s Borea‑Phi‑3.5‑mini‑Instruct‑Common, compiled with the TFMC/imatrix‑dataset‑for‑japanese‑LLM and run via llama.cpp’s CLI.
GGUF conversion of Llama‑3.1‑Swallow‑8B‑Instruct‑v0.5 by tokyotech‑llm, incorporating TFMC/imatrix‑dataset‑for‑japanese‑LLM, with Build/Run instructions for llama.cpp.
An automatically translated Japanese version of the databricks‑dolly‑15k dataset, licensed CC‑BY‑SA‑3.0 and last updated on 2023‑05‑11.
s: Multilinguality, Text Generation, Machine Translation, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: JamC‑QA is a bilingual benchmark of multiple‑choice questions spanning eight Japanese‑culture and knowledge categories, with leaderboard metrics comparing state‑of‑the‑art models.

ReazonSpeech is a free, FLAC‑encoded Japanese speech corpus with transcriptions, offered in five sizes from 8.5 h to 35,000 h, downloadable via Hugging Face under the CDLA‑Sharing‑1.0 license and limited to use under Japan Copyright Act Article 30‑4.
s: Speech & Audio in NLP, Multimodality, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: A NLP‑ready dataset of Wikipedia articles extracted from a January 2024 HTML dump that preserves paragraph structure, metadata (such as disambiguation, sexual, violent flags, templates, timestamps) and accompanying extraction scripts hosted on GitHub.

KokushiMD‑10 is a multilingual benchmark for ten Japanese national healthcare licensing exams that supplies Japanese, English, and mixed‑language splits of text‑ and image‑based multiple‑choice, calculation, and fill‑in‑the‑blank questions across ten professions, each annotated with expert chain‑of‑
Range3’s wikipedia‑ja‑20230101 repository offers Parquet files containing only Japanese Wikipedia text, extracted from the full Wikipedia dataset and generated with Python code.
s: Information Extraction & Text Mining, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: Updated JGLUE dataset card and loading script for a Japanese NLP benchmark (created by Yahoo Japan and Waseda University) that covers text classification (MARC‑ja, JCoLA), sentence‑pair classification (JNLI), and QA (JSQuAD, JCommonsenseQA), with releases linked on GitHub and Hugging Face.

JMMMU is a Japanese multimodal benchmark expanded over tenfold to 1,320 culturally diverse questions (720 culture‑agnostic, 600 culture‑specific) translated by native subject experts, now featuring a public leaderboard.
Dataset of LLM‑generated Japanese queries and answers created from paraphrased Wikipedia text, released under CC‑BY‑SA 4.0.

Provides a Japanese search/QA dataset with per‑query scores computed by five multilingual/Japanese rerankers (e.g., BAAI/bge‑reranker‑v2‑m3, Alibaba‑NLP/gte‑multilingual‑reranker‑base), including average scores for roughly 200 positive and negative example documents per query.
A dataset of 23 million personally gathered Japanese web novels totaling 80.85 billion characters, available only for bona fide machine‑learning use and requiring a detailed access request.

A 525 k‑entry Japanese instruction‑tuning set, automatically translated from ApolloCorpus’s English medical file “medicalPaper_en_qa.json,” offering a high‑quality yet imperfect medical dataset for cautious use in Japanese‑language LLMs.
Loads a 312,396‑row filtered subset of the Oscar 2023 dataset from Hugging Face (if001/oscar_2023_filtered) and provides sample code in the if001/HojiChar_OSCAR_sample GitHub repository.
Range3/wiki40b‑ja hosts a Japanese subset of the wiki40b dataset as three Parquet files produced by a Python data‑processing pipeline.
s: Information Extraction & Text Mining, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: cc100-ja is a collection of the Japanese portion of the cc100 dataset, provided as sharded Parquet files.
s: Information Extraction & Text Mining, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: A user‑friendly, deduplicated CSV dataset of public‑domain Japanese texts from Aozora Bunko, processed with globis‑org/aozorabunko‑extractor and cleaned for modern‑Japanese machine‑learning use.

JMMLU is a Japanese Massive Multitask Language Understanding Benchmark featuring 7,536 teacher‑crafted questions across 56 subjects, including professional medicine, psychology, accounting, philosophy, and diverse high‑school disciplines.
s: Multilinguality, Text Generation, Machine Translation, Language Models

d: JAMMEval is a distilled benchmark of seven Japanese VQA datasets, refined with two rounds of human annotation to remove ambiguities and non‑visual questions, delivering reliable evaluation of vision‑language models on multimodal Japanese tasks.

A ~10,000‑sample Japanese instruction‑tuning dataset, produced by applying Magpie’s method to nvidia/Nemotron‑4‑340B‑Instruct via DeepInfra, includes the creation code but no post‑filtering, so some low‑quality records may be present.

Restructured reupload of the Galgame VisualNovel dataset (OOPPEENN/56697375616C4E6F76656C5F44617461736574) for efficient Hugging Face datasets loading, preserving all original audio/text and providing an extraction script with multiple game‑subset options.

Galgame_Speech_SER_16kHz is a 3.7 million‑file (5,353‑hour, 104 GB) emotional speech dataset derived from Galgame_Speech_ASR_16kHz, annotated by a local LLM, released under GNU GPL v3.0 with commercial use prohibited, and requiring models trained on it to be open‑source without mandatory citation.
Dataset of dialogues and lore from the Fate/Stay Night character “Emilia”, formatted for training and evaluating conversational language models.

A 20,800‑file WAV dataset of 195 Japanese AItuber personas—including reference, original, descriptive, and emotional utterances—is supplied with detailed persona and voice metadata, ready for retrieval via data‑science APIs.

Japanese Wikipedia sentences are transformed into various embeddings and a FAISS index, offering a Hugging Face Space demo, conversion scripts, and evaluations of search, Q&A, and OpenAI text‑embedding‑3‑small for RAG; embeddings are OpenAI‑licensed, others CC‑BY‑SA‑4.0.
A CC‑0 Japanese text‑image dataset, built from ~80 k words/short sentences via GPT‑OSS‑20B, rendered into 1 M and 100 k images with Pillow/Python using the Noto Sans JP font.

Hachi‑Alpaca is a Japanese synthetic dataset created from Stanford Alpaca with ‑mistralai/Mixtral‑8x22B‑Instruct‑v0.1, cleaned by that model, served on Deepinfra, and released under the Apache‑2.0 license for Alpaca‑jp research.

STAIR‑Captions, released in 2017, offers 820,310 Japanese captions for caption generation, multimodal retrieval, and image generation, complete with detailed annotations, metadata, and a Creative Commons BY‑4.0 license.
Japanese Alpaca Data, derived from masa3141’s Japanese Alpaca‑LoRA work, with a reference to the original repository for additional details.

JFWIR is a 64‑million‑pair Japanese information‑retrieval dataset built from fine‑web educational crawls, offering seven query types per document, hard negatives, and demonstrably better benchmark performance than prior baselines.
JA‑VG‑VQA‑500 is a 500‑sample subset of the Japanese Visual Genome VQA dataset, licensed CC BY 4.0, used to benchmark EvoVLM‑JP‑v1‑7B.
s: Visual Data in NLP, Multimodality, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: AI‑generated anime illustrations with English prompts and Phi‑3 Vision‑derived captions (English and Japanese) released into the public domain for free use.
s: Captioning, Text Generation, Multimodality, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: Japanese Anime Speech Dataset offers 73,004 audio‑text pairs (110 hours total, evolving from V1 to V5) to enhance ASR models such as OpenAI’s Whisper, available under an open license for all uses with credit appreciated.
s: Speech Recognition, Text Generation, Speech & Audio in NLP, Multimodality, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: JaQuAD is a 2022 Japanese QA dataset of 39,696 SQuAD‑style extractive pairs from Wikipedia, totaling 73.2 MB, that achieves 78.92 % F1 (63.38 % EM) when fine‑tuned with BERT‑Japanese.
s: Natural Language Interfaces, Question Answering, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: Japanese instruction‑chat dataset for fine‑tuning LLMs (e.g., with LoRA), 9 M+ samples, recently updated to drop licensed Alpaca data, clean Wikipedia and ALT outputs, and released under CC‑BY‑SA 4.0.

Ranking LLMs on how accurately they translate Japanese visual novels into English, the VNTL leaderboard uses cosine‑similarity against 256 reference translations (with chrF reported but not ranked) and reports preliminary results that outperform tools such as Sugoi Translator, Google Translate, Papa
Japanese‑translated HelpSteer2, NVIDIA’s trial dataset for SteerLM used in Nemotron‑4‑430B‑Reward, along with links to alignment and training resources.

A high‑quality synthetic Japanese math problem dataset with verified chain‑of‑thought reasoning, built by translating PRM800K and GSM8K via Qwen2‑7B‑Instruct and filtering for correctness, available through the Hugging Face datasets library.

EDINET‑Bench is a Japanese financial benchmark that evaluates LLMs on tasks such as accounting fraud detection, earnings forecasting, and industry prediction using ten years of EDINET‑API disclosed reports, with construction and evaluation code provided and the dataset relicensed to PDL 1.0.

alpaca_jp_python is a Japanese synthetic Alpaca dataset created with mistralai/Mixtral‑8x22B‑Instruct‑v0.1, cleaned by the same model, hosted on Deepinfra, and released under the HachiML Japanese Apache 2.0 license.

18,612 Japanese‑translated Python instruction–response pairs—generated with GPT‑4o‑mini, preserving original English prompts, code, and examples—offer diverse coding tasks for training, fine‑tuning, chatbots, research, and education, all released under MIT license.
JCommonsenseQA is a Japanese, multiple‑choice dataset for commonsense reasoning—an adaptation of CommonsenseQA—licensed under CC BY‑SA 4.0 and cited as doi:10.5715/jnlp.30.63.
s: Reasoning, Commonsense Reasoning, Semantic Text Processing

d: A MangaOCR dataset comprising roughly 209 K narrative text instances from the Manga109 and manga onomatopoeia collections, featuring diverse visual styles and layouts.

This repository hosts a Japanese translation of the OpenAssistant/oasst1 dataset, including auto‑translated entries with error flags, roughly 2,000 manual corrections, a chat‑formatted subset, and scripts to convert the data into instruction‑output pairs for fine‑tuning.

JSICK is a Japanese NLI and STS dataset translated from the SICK corpus, featuring a stress‑test set that scrambles and rephrases sentence pairs to evaluate models’ sensitivity to word order and case particles for advanced multilingual compositional inference.
Large compressed JSON‑Lines dataset of anonymous 2ch.sc/2ch.net threads, including thread IDs, titles, board and region details, reply counts, and full post metadata (author, mail, date, content).

A Japanese web collection of 56 million documents, 110 B characters and 249 million images used to train large vision‑language models—offering a momiji_generator for data population, OBELICS‑style visualization, and a sample model (Heron‑NVILA‑Lite).
s: Visual Data in NLP, Multimodality, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: JHumanEval is a hand‑translated Japanese version of the HumanEval benchmark, providing 164 Python programming problems with parallel English and Japanese comments to evaluate Japanese‑LLM code generation while preserving the original English errors.
s: Multilinguality, Text Generation, Machine Translation, Language Models, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: A 2010 Japanese Web Corpus, uploaded to HuggingFace and licensed for research per the 2009 copyright reform, includes automatically punctuated text from morphology-based parsing and conversion scripts.

Syosetu711K is a Japanese dataset of ~711,700 novels scraped from 小説家になろう on March 26‑27 2023, providing full text and metadata (title, author, NCode, synopsis, etc.) for unsupervised text generation and classification tasks.

JAQKET is a Japanese open‑domain QA dataset derived from Wikipedia, offering version 1.0 with multiple‑choice quiz questions (13,061 training, 271 validation examples) and version 2.0 with only question prompts requiring extracted answers (2,154 training, 1,164 validation), designed to facilitate re
s: Natural Language Interfaces, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: Japanese instruction dataset with 632,636 multi‑turn samples (~6.35 B tokens) and GPT‑OSS‑120b reasoning traces for open‑ended, math, writing, and MCQA tasks across 135 finance topics plus 20 general ones, designed to fine‑tune LLM reasoning in finance.

A Japanese QA dataset for evaluating Retrieval‑Augmented Generation (RAG), built from JAQKET questions and Wikipedia passages with gold retrieval‑relevance labels, released on HuggingFace and GitHub and scored primarily by nDCG@10.
s: Natural Language Interfaces, Information Retrieval, Question Answering

d: Converted Japanese datasets into SentenceTransformers‑friendly columns, filtering examples by Rerank scores (≥0.7 positive, ≤0.3 negative) from multiple HuggingFace sources to support contrastive learning while respecting the original licenses.
Dataset of Japanese e‑Gov laws, each entry providing number, title, unique ID, effective date, and full text, deduplicated to the most recent effective version as of August 1, 2023.

Rakuda supplies 40 Japanese questions—open‑ended for history, society, and government, and specific for geography—for benchmarking Japanese AI assistants, comparable to vicuna‑eval, and can be loaded with `datasets.load_dataset`.
A Japanese instruction dataset created by manually reviewing and editing outputs from cyberagent/calm2-7b-chat (see the linked article for details).
A Japanese news corpus from July–October 2024 extracted from Common Crawl’s news subset using Uzushio with pipeline_03a.conf filtering settings.
Dataset card for the “wikipedia-ja-20230720” Japanese Wikipedia snapshot.

Dataset card for the JGLUE dataset used in the book “Large Language Model Introduction,” sourced from the original repo, with code licensed CC BY‑SA 4.0, data under the distributor’s license, citing Kurihara & Kawahara (in Japanese), and built on Shunsuke Kitada’s repository.
JA‑Multi‑Image‑VQA is a 39‑image, 55‑question dataset with manually crafted Japanese Q&A for multi‑image VQA, accessed via load_dataset, licensed Apache 2.0 for text (images are restricted).

Galgame_Speech_ASR_16kHz is a 16 kHz ASR dataset with 3.75 million pairs (≈5,354 h), derived from Galgame_Dataset, released under GPL v3.0 with commercial use prohibited and requiring any trained models to be open‑source (citation optional).

A Japanese chat dataset for instruction‑response fine‑tuning (e.g., via LoRA), derived from llm‑japanese‑dataset by excluding Japanese‑English translation examples, offering 1.8–2.5 million entries across versioned releases and licensed under CC‑BY‑SA 4.0 with citation DOI 10.1109/BigData59044.2023.

The repository hosts an 81‑year‑old Japanese woman’s voice corpus—raw and noise‑reduced WAV files, phoneme and kana labels—available via a Google Drive link, freely usable for research and commercial speech synthesis with attribution, but disallowing direct file links or pornographic use.

Japanese Explainable Multi‑hop Question Answering dataset featuring questions, answers, and step‑by‑step derivations linking Wikipedia articles, with updated derivation formatting and multiple version releases.
Japanese Wikipedia Q&A dataset with LLM‑generated questions and answers from DeepSeek‑R1‑Distill‑Qwen‑32B‑Japanese, released under CC‑BY‑SA 4.0.

A high‑quality dataset of synthetic inference Q&A pairs, each featuring a <think>‑wrapped reasoning process and final answer, supplied in JSON with tags for question type, answer format, and conversation genre, intended for fine‑tuning a reasoning model.

KokoroChat is the largest Japanese psychological counseling dialogue dataset—6,589 role‑playing sessions by 480 trained counselors, averaging 91 utterances each—featuring rich, long‑form conversations, detailed 20‑dimensional client feedback, and supports research in empathetic response generation,
LLM‑jp provides a Japanese instruction‑tuning dataset, DeepL‑translated from an English subset of oasst2 (derived from kunishou/oasst2‑135k‑ja) and compiled by Kiyomaru and Kodama.
Japanese anime speech dataset, split from the original `joujiboi/japanese-anime-speech-v2` collection.
Japanese XL‑Sum subset filtered via PaLM‑2 15‑gram overlap, containing 4,215 training, 758 validation, and 766 test examples.

108 k Japanese instruction‑response pairs created with the BARE method, combining Sarashina2‑70B for queries, Qwen3‑235B‑A22B for answers, and filtered by Multilingual‑E5‑Large and Phi‑4, delivered in JSON Lines format.
A simple dataset of Zundamon character settings—compiled from online sources and admin data—for testing character‑LLMs, provided in zmnjp.jsonl and zmn.jsonl formats under a specified license.
s: Vocabulary, Dictionary, and Language Input Method

d: A 97,269‑record Japanese dialogue dataset generated by applying Magpie’s method to weblab‑GENIAC/Tanuki‑8B‑dpo‑v1.0, without post‑filtering, so it may contain low‑quality entries.

The Databricks‑dolly‑15k‑ja dataset is a DeepL‑translated Japanese version of databricks‑dolly‑15k for instruction tuning, created by Japan’s LLM‑jp project and authored by Hirokazu Kiyomaru, Hiroshi Matsuda, Jun Suzuki, Namgi Han, Saku Sugawara, Shota Sasaki, Shuhei Kurita, Taishi Nakamura, Takashi

oasst1‑21k‑ja is a Japanese instruction‑tuning dataset derived by DeepL from the English OASST1 subset, created through the LLM‑jp collaborative project in Japan; contact llm‑jp@nii.ac.jp and authors include Kiyomaru, Matsuda, Suzuki, Han, Sugawara, Sasaki, Kurita, Nakamura, Kodama, and Okamoto.
Japanese instruction dataset of 6,259 manually annotated instruction–response pairs extracted from CohereForAI/aya_dataset, usable via Python’s datasets.load_dataset().

A dataset card for the SNOW T15/T23 Japanese simplification corpus, containing 50 k manually aligned original, simplified Japanese (≤2 k‑word vocab), and English translation records, plus a 35 k expansion set, for use in Japanese‑English text simplification and translation.
A CC‑BY‑SA 3.0 licensed dataset for Japanese named‑entity extraction derived from Wikipedia, hosted at https://github.com/stockmarkteam/ner-wikipedia-dataset/ and developed by Stockmark Inc.
s: Information Extraction & Text Mining, Named Entity Recognition, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: A 190‑million‑pair JSONL dataset for Japanese kana‑to‑kanji conversion that powers the zenz‑v2.5 medium, small, and xsmall models and includes an AJIMEE‑Bench evaluation corpus.

A synthetic 195‑entry AI‑VTuber persona dataset generated with Kimi‑K2.5 via the SDG‑LOOM pipeline, providing concept documents, system prompts, and theme lists for characters defined by six seed parameters (genre, personality, age, gender expression, visual motif, speech style) across about 25 genr

AnswerCarefully Dataset supplies Japanese and multilingual data for commercial or non‑commercial LLM safety enhancement, prohibits any other use—including safety circumvention—allows derivative works with attribution, and carries a creator disclaimer of non‑liability for harms or service changes.

Sentence‑aligned Japanese web novel chapters and English fan translations (106 K chapters, V2), with updated alignment, added series metadata, retained titles, no quality filtering, licensed Apache 2.0 under fair‑use, and a HuggingFace‑based takedown procedure.

A dataset card for llm‑book/wrime‑sentiment offering a binary Japanese sentiment analysis set derived from WRIME, labeled as positive or negative based on Avg. Readers_Sentiment (with an option to include neutral cases), and intended as sample data for the book “Introduction to Large Language Models

A cleaned Japanese web corpus (e.g., mc4‑ja) clustered into roughly 10 000 groups by unsupervised learning, usable for lawful analysis, with only some files in Parquet format listed in the “out” folder and downloadable via Git LFS.

BBH‑ja provides a Japanese translation of the BIG‑Bench Hard dataset, offering evaluation problems in JSON‑L (input, correct target) and Chain‑of‑Thought prompts in YAML (input, target), translated using the PLaMo model.

A dataset of 5,000 Japanese texts from Aozora Bunko scored for difficulty with jReadability, providing overall, kanji, lexical and detailed readability metrics for curriculum design and adaptive learning.
COVID‑19 Japanese Twitter dataset supplies Japanese tweet IDs and assessment codes (63–68) indicating COVID‑19 relevance and fact/opinion status, enabling text‑classification research.

A dataset of 64,139 Japanese names labeled with biological gender—presented in kanji, hiragana, and romaji—whose 44.9 k training, 6.41 k validation, and 12.8 k test split earned acceptance at ISDA’23.
Japanese Common Voice 17.0 subset filtered to 649 client IDs with 30–300 samples each, split 8:2 into train/validation, batched in 1,000‑sample Parquet files, totaling 45,668 samples (CC0 licensed).

The WRIME dataset is a Japanese collection of 42,200 posts annotated with Plutchik’s eight emotions for the writer, three readers, and their averages, structured into 40 k‑train, 1.2 k‑validation, and 2 k‑test splits for sentiment‑analysis tasks.
s: Information Extraction & Text Mining, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: JSNLI is a Japanese translation of the SNLI natural‑language‑inference benchmark, provided as TSV files of morpheme‑segmented premise‑hypothesis–label triples in filtered and unfiltered training and validation splits, licensed under CC BY‑SA 4.0.
Extracts the “content” field text from OSCOR‑2301‑ja JSON files, along with a Python script that pulls these values from every JSON in a specified folder.

J-HARD‑TTS‑Eval benchmarks the robustness of autoregressive Japanese TTS models on short‑sequence stability, repetition handling, and context completion, with datasets available via Hugging Face (short, repetition, rhyme, continuation).

A 5.2 k‑item instruction dataset focused on code, now adding 180 JaxTon and professional‑Java entries, comprising 1,050 code‑generation, 150 code‑behavior‑check and 4,000 code‑fix records, assembled from commercially licensed learning content that has been translated into Japanese and fully authoriz

A dataset that quantifies the intelligence gap between voice‑ and text‑input for voice‑language models, consisting of audio synthesized from the Elyza‑tasks‑100, M‑IFEval, and JamC‑QA benchmarks into four subsets, with text under CC‑BY‑SA 4.0 and audio restricted to non‑commercial, non‑redistributab
Fork of the AozoRABunko‑clean dataset, filtered to include only rows where meta['文字遣い種別'] equals '新字新仮名'.
Japanese translation of NVIDIA’s HelpSteer trial dataset for testing and aligning SteerLM, accompanied by links to SteerLM training resources.
Dataset card detailing the Japanese‑language variant “mc4‑ja‑filter‑ja‑normal”, with additional information pending.
llm‑jp‑instructions is a manually curated Japanese instruction dataset (v1.0) offering train, dev, and test splits accessible through load_dataset.

A 409‑hour Japanese eroge voice dataset processed with 2‑pass loudnorm (‑23 LUFS, ‑1 dB peak, 11 LRA), transcribed by litagin/anime-whisper, anonymized, stored as WebDataset (FLAC, JSON, TXT), largely featuring female voices with potential AI transcription errors, and MIT‑licensed for academic resea

Malum‑230 is a human‑crafted Japanese dataset of multi‑turn conversations and passages designed for logical reasoning, suitable for pre‑ and post‑training and evaluated on Japanese MT‑Bench with Qwen2.5‑7B.
Preprocessed Japanese Callhome audio is converted to WAV with companion JSON metadata arrays and RTTM files for evaluation, featuring speakers such as callhome_jpn_0.

A 10,000‑sample paired dataset of ~30 hours of Japanese audio with aligned Simplified Chinese text in Parquet format (CC BY 4.0), designed for speech‑to‑text translation and multilingual ASR+MT research.
Neologism dictionary based on the language resources on the Web for mecab-ipadic
s: Vocabulary, Dictionary, and Language Input Method

d: JMMMU‑Pro is a Japanese multimodal benchmark built via Vibe Construction—combining generative modeling and human verification—to generate low‑cost, high‑quality image‑QA data that exposes open‑source LMMs’ shortcomings and steers future VQA research.

Livedoor News Corpus offers a Japanese news article dataset, split into 5 894 training, 737 validation, and 736 test instances, cleaned of HTML tags and released under a Creative Commons Attribution‑NoDerivs license.

266‑hour Japanese audio dataset from the Emilia collection, pre‑filtered by Facebook Audio Aesthetics and classified via Scribe v1/ElevenLabs STT, hosted on Hugging Face and open for collaboration on Discord.

Japanese‑only MGE‑universe stories pre‑approved and chronologically sorted, each with metadata (chapter, title, author, summary, tags, views, votes, update date, byte/token lengths) and up to 500 k raw text characters ready for further processing.

Japanese role‑play dataset expanded from ~19,800 to ~39,600 examples using GPT‑4o‑mini, formatted with added system messages, licensed CC‑BY‑NC‑SA 4.0 and restricted from building models that compete with OpenAI’s services.
Dataset card for “oscar2301‑ja‑filter‑ja‑normal,” a Japanese filtered and normal subset of the Oscar corpus.

Dataset of Japanese role‑play dialogues filtered to include only multi‑poster records of sufficient length, normalized poster names, and balanced top speakers, intended for machine‑learning applications.
Reformatted Japanese subset of the Wiki40B dataset, authored by Mandy Guo, Zihang Dai, and Denny Vrandečić.

Repository providing the 1.56 B‑token pre‑training dataset (from various licensed public corpora) used to train the AKU‑d_ms‑0.5B‑chat‑v0.1 model in the AKU series, with accompanying processing scripts.
The “Regexp.ja” Wiki page in the MeCab‑IPADIC‑Neologd repository explains Japanese regex rules for stripping unnecessary punctuation and normalizing text.

Tengentoppa corpus is a large, JSON‑formatted Japanese instruction‑following dataset assembled from 16 diverse public datasets, linked to a processor script and derived from numerous open‑source sources.
AnimuSubtitle‑JP is a Japanese subtitle dataset in Advanced SubStation Alpha format, usable via the Python ‘ass’ library or editors like Aegisub, and licensed under ODC‑By.

Filtered Japanese/Chinese bilingual data from CCMatrix v1 stripped of abnormal pairs via regex and length checks, pruned to a LaBSE semantic similarity of 0.6, and all Traditional Chinese text converted to Simplified Chinese with zhconv.
Chat‑formatted ShareGPT dataset derived from oasst1‑89k‑ja, ideal for multi‑turn fine‑tuning but with long token sequences that require substantial compute, available on GitHub and HuggingFace.

AutoWikiQA is the largest free Japanese QA dataset, providing over 2.3 million handcrafted‑filtered question–answer pairs automatically generated from Wikipedia with Swallow‑MX and LLMs (without rule‑based templates) to support knowledge‑teaching and retrieval‑augmented generation applications.

RealPersonaChat is a 14,000‑utterance Japanese conversation corpus that pairs dialogue records with speaker persona, BigFive personality scores, demographic data, and evaluative ratings (1–5), accessible through Hugging Face’s `load_dataset` but requiring strict privacy and non‑impersonation complia
Editor for VOICEVOX, a high-quality text-to-speech software that can be used for free.

Four Japanese‑centric image‑classification tasks—jafood101 (101 dishes), jaflower30 (30 flowers), jafacility20 (20 facilities), and jalandmark10 (10 landmarks)—are bundled in a dataset by Recruit Co. under a CC‑BY‑4.0 license.
A 5‑million‑sentence dataset of clean Japanese full‑sentence context, ideal for unsupervised semantic similarity learning.

J‑ResearchCorpus supplies about 39 million characters of high‑quality Japanese research paper text drawn from 1,343 CC‑BY‑4.0 sources—including NLP2024 conference papers and journal articles—ready for language‑model pre‑training and RAG use.

Provides a Japanese RAG benchmark across five industry domains—finance, telecom, manufacturing, public, retail—by publishing datasets, an automatic evaluation framework, and comparative results for models such as Claude 3.5‑Sonnet, GPT‑4o, and others.

A Japanese hard‑negative mining dataset derived from the MS MARCO translation, filtered and re‑ranked with BAAI/bge‑reranker‑v2‑m3 and scored by japanese‑splade‑base‑v1‑mmarco‑only, shows statistically significant higher positive rates than mMARCO(ja) at 0.7/0.8 thresholds (≈ 1.3–1.7 % improvement).
Cosmopedia Japanese data has been expanded to 100 k entries (with prompt translations) and is available as a Hugging Face dataset and in the repo.
Pre‑training dataset for shisa‑base‑7b‑v1, built from a DSIR sample of MADLAD‑400 Japanese/English tokens in a 90 %/10 % ratio.

alpaca_jp_math is a Japanese synthetic math dataset produced with Stanford Alpaca and mistralai/Mixtral‑8x22B‑Instruct‑v0.1, cleaned by a prompt that validates that program‑computed and text outputs agree to the third decimal, and released under HachiML Apache 2.0.

39,600 synthetic Japanese role‑play conversations of 5–10 turns each, produced with gpt‑4o‑mini and annotated with genre, age‑tag, world/scene/user/assistant settings and tone, provided in OpenAI messages format under a CC‑BY‑NC‑SA 4.0 license that forbids competing models with OpenAI.

WildGuardTestJP is a Japanese‑language evaluation dataset of 1,725 samples, faithfully translated from WildGuardTest through a multi‑stage refinement pipeline (Seed‑X‑PPO‑7B, gpt‑oss‑120b, Qwen2.5‑72B‑Instruct, gemma‑3‑27b‑it) and released on Hugging Face under the ODC‑BY license.
Dataset card for the ja‑vicuna‑qa‑benchmark used in the book “Introduction to Large‑Scale LLM II” and created by llm‑jp‑eval for cross‑dataset Japanese LLM evaluation (Apache 2.0).
s: Natural Language Interfaces, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: Japanese RAG Generator Benchmark (J‑RAGBench) supplies a multi‑category QA dataset—covering Integration, Reasoning, Logical, Table, and Abstention—designed to evaluate Japanese RAG generators, built with human effort and GPT‑4.1, and released under CC BY‑SA 4.0.

Japanese Document Question Answering (JDocQA) is a large‑scale PDF‑based QA dataset with 5,504 documents and 11,600 annotated Japanese Q&A pairs covering yes/no, factoid, numerical, and open‑ended questions, including unanswerable instances, designed to evaluate complex comprehension of visual and t

A non‑official dataset of Hololive VTuber Sakura Miko’s voice for speech‑recognition research, organized by source platform and split into train/test folders, licensed under Hololive’s fan‑creation guidelines with all rights held by Cover Corporation.

Japanese LLaVA Pretrain is a DeepL‑transferred localized version of the original LLaVA Pretrain dataset, keeping the CC‑3M and BLIP licenses, and is aimed at researchers and hobbyists building Japanese‑language multimodal models and chatbots.

Manually extracted QC‑checked FAQ dataset from Japanese government agency websites, licensed CC‑BY‑4.0, presented in QA format with source URLs for LLM instruction‑tuning and RAG testing, yet subject to PDF‑to‑text conversion quirks and strong governmental perspectives.
Japanese translation of a subset of the hh‑rlhf human preference dataset, produced by the Japan‑based LLM‑jp collaborative project using DeepL.

Auto‑generated multi‑turn Q&A dataset: initial questions drawn from open sources, all subsequent replies synthesized by Calm3‑22b, built with Tokyo Tech’s TSUBAME4.0, and released under the listed licenses.

A processed Japanese Stack Exchange QA dataset of 28,428 English‑to‑Japanese question–answer pairs, providing default and simple subsets with post and answer metadata (IDs, accepted/popular answers, scores, tags, titles, bodies), readable via the Hugging Face `datasets` library and licensed under CC
s: Natural Language Interfaces, Annotation and Dataset Development

d: A 96k‑sample dataset applying Magpie to Tanuki‑8B, annotated with difficulty, quality, and task‑category labels by cyberagent/calm3‑22b‑chat.
A Voicevox‑generated artificial voice dataset comprising 445,793 .wav files from the ITA, つくよみちゃん, and ROHAN corpora, totaling 577 h 51 m 23 s of audio.
a Japanese Input Method Editor designed for multi-platform
s: Syntactic Text Processing, Vocabulary, Dictionary, and Language Input Method

d: A cleaned subset of the livedoor news corpus (licensed CC BY‑ND 2.1 JP) used in the book “Introduction to Large Language Models,” described by the llm‑book/ner‑wikinews‑dataset card.

Japanese Wikipedia Human Retrieval dataset records trained workers’ manual Wikipedia‑based retrievals for 838 answered and 433 unanswered Japanese question‑answer pairs, defined by Yusuke Oda and collected, checked, and formatted by Baobab Inc.
Hugging Face mirror of the ABEJA‑CC‑JA open‑data set from AWS (https://registry.opendata.aws/abeja-cc-ja/) with details in Abeja’s technical blog.
Japanese‑Heron‑Bench supplies 21 culturally‑tagged Japanese images with 102 Q&A across Conversation, Detail, and Complex categories, benchmarked for Japanese vision‑language models.

A ~1,000‑item Japanese role‑play instruction set, produced by applying the Synthetic‑JP‑Roleplay‑Instruction‑Nemotron‑4 Magpie method to NVIDIA/Nemotron‑4‑340B‑Instruct using DeepInfra, contains unfiltered data that may include low‑quality entries.
Japanese subset of the Guanaco dataset, comparable to repos such as inu‑ai/alpaca‑guanaco‑japanese‑gpt‑1b.
A dataset of summary pairs derived from long texts in the Aozora Bunko corpus (globis‑university/aozorabunko-clean) under a CC BY 4.0 license.

A 11,808‑sample dataset of multi‑turn conversations about Japanese images sourced from ThePioneer/japanese‑photos, partially filtered with Azure OpenAI, licensed under CC‑BY‑4.0 and subject to OpenAI terms.
A modified version of kunishou/hh‑rlhf‑49k‑ja that excludes entries with ng_translation = 1, referencing the original dataset.
A Nekopara audio dataset providing 44.1 kHz recordings labeled by character name, volume, original file name, transcription, and an is_adult flag that requires careful use for NSFW filtering.

A partial dataset of *Project Sekai* vocal tracks for 草薙寧々 (source CV Machico) including a nene_org.txt label file, with plans to expand and standardise the data, and a call to star the repo, share ideas and join a QQ group for the full collection.
s: Vocabulary, Dictionary, and Language Input Method

d: JP Voice‑Text Dataset for Girls Front Line Waifus: 12,508 recordings (20.9 h) of single‑actor female characters, ideal for ASR/ASV fine‑tuning and evaluation.
Japanese voice‑text dataset for Fate/Grand Order characters, 30,800 clips (≈66.4 h total) featuring single‑actor recordings ideal for ASR/ASV fine‑tuning and evaluation.
A Japanese chat‑format dataset, **oasst2‑chat‑68k‑ja**, derived from DeepL‑translated OpenAssistant/oasst2, with provided code to convert it into Instruction/Output pairs for fine‑tuning.

A ChatGPT‑style “ShareGPT” formatted dataset (oasst2‑135k‑ja) ready for multi‑turn fine‑tuning, though each record’s long token length demands substantial compute resources, with guidance linked via the OpenAssistant Hugging Face repository.
